items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 23 at 2:43PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Additional snow
accumulations up to one inch.

* WHERE...Bering Strait Coast.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT this afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility.
items.instruction:
Slow down and use caution while traveling. Visit 511.alaska.gov or
call 5 1 1 for the latest road conditions.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 23 at 2:43PM AKDT until April 23 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Wintry mix expected. Additional snow accumulations up to
one inch. Winds gusting as high as 45 mph.

* WHERE...St Lawrence Island.

* WHEN...Until 10 PM AKDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult. Areas of blowing snow
could significantly reduce visibility. Strong winds could result
in power outages.
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 23 at 2:34PM AKDT until April 23 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Additional snow
accumulations up to one inch.

* WHERE...Point Lay.

* WHEN...Until 10 PM AKDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Areas of blowing snow could
reduce visibility.
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 23 at 12:27PM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 35 to 45 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: High Wind Warning issued April 23 at 12:27PM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 40 to 50 mph with gusts up to 75 mph.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.areas: Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 23 at 12:27PM AKDT until April 25 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Southeast winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 50 mph
expected.

* WHERE...Delta Junction.

* WHEN...From 4 AM Friday to 4 AM AKDT Saturday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...There may be a brief decrease in wind speeds
Friday afternoon.
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Flood Watch issued April 23 at 12:21PM AKDT until April 25 at 11:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by rain and snowmelt continues to be
possible.

* WHERE...The following areas, Central Susitna Valley, Southern
Susitna Valley and Western Susitna Valley.  This includes the
communities of Skwentna, Willow, Talkeetna, Trapper Creek, And
Petersville.

* WHEN...Through Saturday morning.

* IMPACTS...Excessive runoff may result in flooding of rivers,
creeks, streams, other low-lying and flood-prone locations. Storm
drains and ditches may be clogged by ice. Flooding may occu
items.areas: Western Susitna Valley; Southern Susitna Valley; Central Susitna Valley
items.instruction:
Persons living along streams should stay alert and be prepared to
take action if a warning or advisory is issued, or flooding is
observed.

You should monitor later forecasts and be alert for possible Flood

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 23 at 5:14AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
A series of frontal systems will lift a plume of moisture from the
tropical Pacific into Prince William Sound and along the coastal
mountains through Friday. The bulk of this precipitation will
fall in the form of heavy wet snow through Thompson Pass as
temperatures hover between 29 and 33 degrees. 8 to 16 inches of
snow is possible through Saturday morning, with the heaviest snow
likely Thursday morning through Thursday afternoon and again early
Friday morning through Friday afternoon. Southeas
